title: IEA Global EV Outlook 2024 public extract
scope_note: This file archives only the public numeric anchors and short textual extracts that were visible on IEA public pages during the 2026-03-22 audit. It is not a raw IEA chart CSV.

2020.note: Compound-growth bridge from the public 2018 and 2023 GEVO-2024 sales-share anchors.

APS_2030.note: Derived from the public GEVO-2024 statements that electric LDV sales reach 43 million in 2030 and account for 40% of LDV sales in STEPS, while APS reaches 47 million in 2030.

public_extracts.label: Historical sales share
public_extracts.extract: Electric cars accounted for around 18% of all cars sold in 2023, up from 14% in 2022 and only 2% 5 years earlier, in 2018. Battery electric cars accounted for 70% of the electric car stock in 2023.

public_extracts.label: STEPS LDV anchor
public_extracts.extract: Global electric light-duty vehicle sales are set to reach over 43 million in 2030 and 60 million in 2035 based on current policy settings, accounting for 40% of total LDV sales in 2030 and almost 55% by 2035.

public_extracts.label: APS LDV volume anchor
public_extracts.extract: In the APS, sales of electric LDVs reach 47 million in 2030 and 75 million in 2035, representing two-thirds of sales in 2035.

public_extracts.label: NZE anchor
public_extracts.extract: In the NZE Scenario, EV sales shares accelerate much more quickly in this decade, reaching about 65% in 2030 and 95% in 2035. 100% of light vehicle sales, including 2/3Ws, cars and vans, are zero-emission vehicles by 2035.
